questions.q: Sambhaji was the son and successor of which founder of the Maratha kingdom?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was the eldest son of Chhatrapati Shivaji, founder of the Maratha kingdom.

questions.q: Sambhaji belonged to which royal clan of the Marathas?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji belonged to the Bhonsle clan, the dynasty founded by his father Shivaji.

questions.q: In which year did Sambhaji become ruler of the Maratha kingdom?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji formally became ruler of the Maratha kingdom in 1681, after the death of his father.

questions.q: Against which Mughal emperor did Sambhaji wage continuous war?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji fought a long war against the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb, who had moved to the Deccan.

questions.q: What was the fate of Sambhaji in 1689 CE?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was captured by Mughal forces and executed in 1689, becoming a martyr in Maratha lore.

questions.q: Sambhaji's resistance against the Mughals is part of the heritage of which Indian power?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji's defiance of the Mughals is celebrated as part of the legacy of the Maratha Empire.

questions.q: Sambhaji ruled during which century CE?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji reigned from 1681 to 1689, placing his rule in the 17th century CE.

questions.q: Sambhaji's father Shivaji had founded a state in which region of India?
questions.explanation: Shivaji founded the Maratha kingdom in western India, in the Deccan region.

questions.q: Sambhaji was the eldest son of Shivaji and the mother who bore him was named what?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji's mother was Saibai, Shivaji's first wife, who died when Sambhaji was young.

questions.q: Sambhaji is best remembered for his role as a what?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji is chiefly remembered as a warrior king who fiercely resisted Mughal expansion.

questions.q: What title, borne by the head of the Maratha state, did Sambhaji hold?
questions.explanation: As the sovereign of the Marathas, Sambhaji held the title Chhatrapati, like his father Shivaji.

questions.q: Sambhaji succeeded to the Maratha throne soon after the death of his father in which year?
questions.explanation: Shivaji died in 1680, and after a brief succession dispute Sambhaji was crowned in 1681.

questions.q: Sambhaji granted refuge to which rebel Mughal prince, son of Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji sheltered Prince Muhammad Akbar, Aurangzeb's rebellious son, which intensified the Mughal-Maratha conflict.

questions.q: Sambhaji was captured by Mughal forces at which place in 1689?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was captured at Sangameshwar in 1689 in a surprise Mughal raid led by Muqarrab Khan.

questions.q: Who succeeded Sambhaji as the Maratha Chhatrapati after his execution?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji's half-brother Rajaram succeeded him and continued the war against the Mughals.

questions.q: Sambhaji was a learned man credited with composing a Sanskrit work titled what?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was a scholar who authored the Sanskrit treatise Budhbhushanam along with other works.

questions.q: During Sambhaji's reign the Marathas clashed with which European power on the Konkan coast?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji campaigned against the Portuguese in Goa and the Konkan during his reign.

questions.q: Sambhaji's son, who later became Chhatrapati and was long a Mughal captive, was named what?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji's son Shahu was captured as a child and later became Chhatrapati, ushering in the era of the Peshwas.

questions.q: Which Mughal general led the raid that captured Sambhaji in 1689?
questions.explanation: Muqarrab Khan led the Mughal force that surprised and captured Sambhaji at Sangameshwar.

questions.q: Sambhaji's reign lasted roughly how many years?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji ruled from 1681 to his execution in 1689, a reign of roughly nine years.

questions.q: Sambhaji is also referred to in Marathi tradition by which honorific epithet?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji is popularly remembered in Maratha tradition by the epithet Dharmaveer, the defender of dharma.

questions.q: Before becoming Chhatrapati, the young Sambhaji had once been sent to the court of which Mughal noble as part of a treaty arrangement?
options: Mirza Raja Jai Singh / the Mughal court
questions.explanation: Under the Treaty of Purandar arrangements, the boy Sambhaji accompanied his father into Mughal service for a time.

questions.q: Sambhaji's coronation as Chhatrapati took place in 1681 at which fort?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was crowned at Raigad in 1681, the same capital fort where his father had been enthroned.

questions.q: Sambhaji was executed near which confluence town in the Pune region in 1689?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was put to death at Tulapur near Vadhu, at the confluence of the Bhima and Indrayani rivers.

questions.q: Sambhaji's chief minister and advisor, later executed alongside him, was the Sanskrit scholar known as what?
questions.explanation: Kavi Kalash, a Brahmin poet and close advisor of Sambhaji, was captured and executed with him.

questions.q: Early in his reign Sambhaji's stepmother Soyarabai had attempted to place which rival on the throne?
questions.explanation: Soyarabai sought to crown her own son Rajaram instead of Sambhaji, sparking a brief succession struggle.

questions.q: Sambhaji launched a naval and land expedition against the Siddis of which island fortress?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji repeatedly attacked the Siddis of Janjira, a powerful island fortress off the Konkan coast.

questions.q: Sambhaji's birth-name as a child was simply Sambhaji, but he was born in 1657 at which fort?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji was born in 1657 at Purandar fort, a stronghold of his father Shivaji.

questions.q: Aurangzeb is said to have vowed not to wear his turban-aigrette again until which goal was achieved, a vow connected to the Sambhaji conflict?
options: The capture of Sambhaji and conquest of the Deccan
questions.explanation: Tradition holds Aurangzeb swore not to wear his ornamented turban until he had subdued the Marathas, including capturing Sambhaji.

questions.q: During Sambhaji's reign the Marathas allied loosely with which southern sultanate state against the Mughals?
questions.explanation: Sambhaji's Marathas found common cause with the Qutb Shahi rulers of Golconda against Aurangzeb's Deccan campaigns.
